ocr.text: Prescribing indicators (World Health Organization, 1993)  All  Hospital  Health centre/clinic
Average number of medicines prescribed per encounter (SD)  4.01 (1.406)  3.27 (1.140)  4.25 (1.400)
% of prescriptions with antibiotics  59.9  48.2  63.7
% of medicines prescribed by generic name  79.2  61.4  83.7
% of encounters with an injection prescribed  22.9  19.8  24.0
% of medicines prescribed from EML  88.1  78.3  90.6
% of prescriptions with drug combinations known to interact  4.9  2.5  5.7
Average number of diagnosis per clinical encounter (SD)  1.69 (0.739)  1.44 (0.600)  1.78 (0.765)
Cause  Smoothing  Age selection regression  Age selection regression  RR < 1 → RR = 1  RR < 1 → RR = 1
Men  Women  Men  Women
All causes  Age + age squared  35+  40+; RR40–44 = 1  35–39
Vascular  Age + age squared  35+  40+  80+
Respiratory  Age + age squared  40+  40+  35–39, 40–44  35–39
External  Age + age squared  35+  40+
The first table presents prescribing indicators from the World Health Organization (1993), showing that the average number of
medicines prescribed per encounter is 4.01 (1.406) overall, 3.27 (1.140) in hospitals, and 4.25 (1.400) in health centres/clinics,
with 59.9% of prescriptions containing antibiotics, 48.2% in hospitals, and 63.7% in health centres/clinics. Furthermore, 79.2%
of medicines are prescribed by generic name, with 61.4% in hospitals and 83.7% in health centres/clinics. The percentage of
encounters with an injection prescribed is 22.9% overall, 19.8% in hospitals, and 24.0% in health centres/clinics, while 88.1%
of medicines prescribed are from the Essential Medicines List (EML), comprising 78.3% in hospitals and 90.6% in health
centres/clinics. Additionally, 4.9% of prescriptions include drug combinations known to interact, with 2.5% in hospitals and
5.7% in health centres/clinics. The average number of diagnoses per clinical encounter is 1.69 (0.739) overall, 1.44 (0.600) in
hospitals, and 1.78 (0.765) in health centres/clinics. The second table outlines causes of mortality with age selection regression,
indicating that for all causes, the smoothing method is applied with age + age squared for men and women aged 35+; for
cancers, the same method applies with RR35–39 = 1 for men aged 35+ and RR40–44 = 1 for women aged 40+. The vascular
category uses age + age squared for men aged 35+ and women aged 40+. Respiratory causes apply age + age squared for both
men and women aged 40+, while external causes use age + age squared with age selections of 35+ for men and 40+ for women.
